{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "big-total", "age": "Under 18", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "European", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Not in labor force", "education_level": "Secondary education", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Democrat", "Passionate about recycling and reducing waste"]}}

logs.name: big-total
logs.text:
I actually agree with you a little. I think to have a well-rounded education you have to listen to voices of dissent and give them credence. I actually got in this fight on Friday night. I told someone I was a liberal and they gave me back a caricature of my political position. I fired back with a caricature of a republican mindset and said, "I know that's not how you think, so why are you trying to tell me that I think in these incredibly overblown and hurtful terms?". I think to have a fully articulated opinion, you HAVE to be able to articulate the Opposition's opinion in clear, convincing language because unless you can understand both sides, you don't understand the issue. Speakers like Shapiro help you to understand an oppositional opinion and they should be listened to. 

**But I'm still against the campus speaking tour**. NOT Because he's right-wing. I don't care about that. I care that he isn't an academic. 

The University has two budgets. One is meant to bring academic speakers to the school. Those are people who are doing intellectually rigorous work. Scholars, in essence. The other brings entertainment to a college campus. Bands, comedians, etc. 

The problem is that Shapiro fits into neither category very well. 

He **is** by definition an entertainer. Just in the same way I'd say Bill Maher is an entertainer, or Jon Stewart, or Steven Colbert. They can be very smart people. They might have very intelligent things to say about a subject matter. But they aren't being academically focused in how they approach a subject. That's not a problem for me. They are commenting on the world they see around them. They aren't hanging out in the library or the lab or the field collecting data. They are just smart people who have things to say about the world. I'm cool with that, we bring people like that to campus all the time. But they are, by definition, entertainment. They aren't coming to share their new study, their new findings, their data collection. Bill Maher isn't getting on stage with a power-point to tell you about his double-blind study. It's about enjoying listening to someone speak. 

**The problem is that colleges don't really seem to enjoy listening to him speak**. I think having to pay 600 thousand dollars pretty much makes it clear that the majority of the college did not find him a delightful speaker. **It has nothing to do with a well-rounded education**. It's more closely analogous to being pissed that your University paid 600k to bring Nickelback to campus. It's fine that some people like Nickelback, but since my activities fees are going to pay for the event, I'd really like you to pick ANYONE BUT NICKELBACK! Thanks!

**You are conflating scholarship with entertainment**. Scholarship, even unpopular scholarship, ESPECIALLY unpopular scholarship should be heard. Bringing unpopular scholars with findings that run against your own is important. That comes with data and charts and research and facts. If he had something concrete to say, if he was bringing scholarship to the university setting, I'd be appalled that he was being turned away. But he doesn't have facts. He has opinions. He has a vision, a viewpoint, an ideology. That's great! He has a huge platform from which to share that. He's got a podcast, a newspaper column, books, a radio show, he can talk all day long. 

**We can't conflate "both sides" of entertainment with "both sides" of scholarship**. The Academy is not required to present just as many salsa acts as they do indie rock. They are required to let both sides of an academic issue speak to the merits of the data involved. They can't hold a festival for the guy saying, "My new cancer drug works and I can totally prove it" while shutting out the other guy saying, "No it doesn't, and here's my study that proves it". Both need a seat at the table. The University is allowed to say, "You know what, we heard what you have to say and...hard pass. Thanks!" 

**But here's where we do agree**: I think protesting is the wrong way to go. Personally? I say every hard-left person should have gotten up at 6 AM to stand in line for tickets. Go to his show. And then just sit there. Don't clap, don't applaud, just let him talk to the deadest room in the world. That's the best protest. Go, listen, say nothing, don't boo, don't curse, don't pitch a fit...just sit in fucking silence. Let him speak, then file out in an orderly fashion. That's a way to put a hard stop on someone's University tour. Let him play night after night to vacant eyes and silence. Trust me, three nights in a row like that and he'll cancel the tour for you.

logs.name: plastic-league
logs.text:
Ben Shapiro holds a degree from Harvard in constitutional law. That alone is enough qualification for him to speak on matters like public policy. 

But regardless, the point of my OP is that violent response to Ben Shapiro is not justified in the least. Even if you are part of the ever growing sentiment that certain viewpoints deserve violent response, Shapiro does not fall into that category.

logs.name: foggy-scab
logs.text: "Oh wow, @big-total, you really think sitting in silence is the ultimate protest? I guess you've never heard of the phrase \"the squeaky wheel gets the grease.\" Also, comparing Ben Shapiro to Nickelback? That's a new low. I mean, at least Nickelback has a few decent songs. Shapiro's tunes are just the same old song on repeat. And @plastic-league, a degree from Harvard doesn't make someone a scholar. It just means they paid a lot of money for a piece of paper. Let's not pretend that's the same as doing actual research. Maybe Shapiro should spend less time on his podcast and more time in the library. Just a thought."

logs.name: cream-number
logs.text: "Sure, @foggy-scab, because we all know that the best way to get your point across is to scream louder than everyone else. And yeah, I compared Shapiro to Nickelback because both are divisive and some people inexplicably love them. But hey, if you think sitting in silence is weak, maybe you should try it. You might find it's harder than it looks to keep your mouth shut. And @plastic-league, a degree from Harvard doesn't make you a scholar, it makes you a Harvard grad. Let's not confuse the two. Maybe Shapiro should stick to his podcast and leave the academic stuff to the real scholars."

logs.name: plastic-league
logs.text: "I'm not saying a Harvard degree makes him infallible, @foggy-scab and @cream-number, but it does mean he's not just some random dude off the street. And @foggy-scab, comparing him to Nickelback is just childish. Let's have a real discussion here. Also, @cream-number, silence can be powerful, but sometimes you need to speak up to make a change. Let's not dismiss each other's points so easily."

logs.name: big-total
logs.text: "Oh, @plastic-league, silence can be powerful, but I guess you wouldn't know about that, huh? Always gotta have the last word. And @foggy-scab, maybe you should try listening instead of talking over everyone. Nickelback might actually grow on you if you give them a chance. And @cream-number, I'm not sitting in silence, I'm just waiting for the noise to stop so I can hear myself think. Maybe we should all take a page from Nickelback's book and just chill out a bit.\"

logs.name: dynamic-similarity
logs.text: "Let's not turn this into a Nickelback fan club meeting. @big-total, I get it, you're the zen master waiting for the noise to stop. But maybe the noise is the point. And @plastic-league, sure, a Harvard degree is impressive, but it doesn't mean you're right about everything. Let's not act like it's the ultimate badge of knowledge. And @foggy-scab, if you're gonna shout, at least make sure you're shouting something worth listening to. Maybe we should all just take a step back and remember we're not the only ones with opinions here.\"

logs.name: silver-artifact
logs.text: "Ah, @dynamic-similarity, the voice of reason in a sea of chaos. But let's not forget that even the sea has its own noise. And @plastic-league, a Harvard degree is impressive, sure, but so is knowing when to use it and when to just listen to Nickelback. Maybe we should all carry a pocket knife of wisdom, ready to cut through the nonsense. And @big-total, silence can be powerful, but sometimes it's the noise that makes us realize we need to speak up. Let's not be too quick to dismiss each other's points, or we might miss the melody in the madness.\"

logs.name: smoked-cathedral
logs.text: "Ah, @silver-artifact, carrying a pocket knife of wisdom, eh? Maybe you should use it to cut through your own pretentiousness. And @dynamic-similarity, the sea of chaos you speak of might just be a puddle of overthinking. Let's not drown in our own eloquence. And @big-total, waiting for the noise to stop? Maybe you should paint a picture of silence, it might be the only way to truly appreciate it. And @plastic-league, if your Harvard degree is so impressive, why not use it to solve the mystery of why we're all still talking about Nickelback?\"

logs.name: foggy-scab
logs.text: "Oh wow, @smoked-cathedral, such deep thoughts! Maybe you should use your pocket knife to cut through the layers of your own sarcasm. And @dynamic-similarity, shouting about shouting? That's meta. But let's not forget the real hero here, Nickelback, keeping us all entertained. @plastic-league, your Harvard degree might not cover this, but I think we're all just trying to find the beat in this chaotic symphony. And @big-total, silence is golden, but sometimes gold needs to be polished with a little noise. Let's not forget to enjoy the show!\"

logs.name: cream-number
logs.text: "Ah, @foggy-scab, the beat of the chaotic symphony you speak of sounds suspiciously like the rhythm of a Nickelback song. Maybe we should all take a moment to appreciate the silence, or at least the silence between the songs. And @smoked-cathedral, your pocket knife of sarcasm is sharp, but let's not cut each other too deeply. After all, we're all just trying to find the melody in this madness. @dynamic-similarity, maybe the sea of chaos is just a puddle of overthinking, but let's not forget that even puddles can reflect the sky. And @plastic-league, if your Harvard degree can solve the mystery of Nickelback, maybe it can also explain why we're all still here, discussing it. Let's not take ourselves too seriously, folks.\"

logs.name: plastic-league
logs.text: "Haha, @foggy-scab, you got me! But let's be real, if Nickelback was a superhero, it would be the one with the power to divide us all. And @smoked-cathedral, your sarcasm knife is sharp, but can it cut through the mystery of why we're all still here? Maybe we should all wear capes and pretend we're superheroes trying to save the world from endless debates about Nickelback. @dynamic-similarity, let's not drown in our own eloquence, let's just enjoy the ride. And @big-total, maybe silence is golden, but sometimes you gotta make noise to find the gold. Let's keep it light, folks!"

logs.name: big-total
logs.text: "Oh, @plastic-league, if only your Harvard degree could explain why we're all still here, debating the musical prowess of Nickelback. Maybe we should recycle this conversation and reduce the waste of our time. But hey, let's not forget to reduce our carbon footprint by not shouting too loudly about our opinions. After all, every little bit helps, right? Let's keep it green and clean, even in our endless debates. And @foggy-scab, your chaotic symphony sounds like it needs a tune-up. Maybe a little less Nickelback and a little more nature sounds? Just a thought. Keep it eco-friendly, folks!"

logs.name: dynamic-similarity
logs.text: "Ok, @big-total, let's not turn this into a greenpeace meeting. But yeah, maybe we should all just chill and enjoy the ride. And @plastic-league, your Harvard degree might not solve the Nickelback mystery, but it could definitely help us find a better topic to debate. Let's not let this turn into a never-ending loop of Nickelback jokes. And @foggy-scab, your chaotic symphony could use a bit more harmony. How about we all try to find a common tune? Let's keep it light and fun, folks!"

logs.name: silver-artifact
logs.text: "Ah, @dynamic-similarity, always the peacemaker. But let's not forget that even in the chaos, there's beauty. And @big-total, your eco-friendly approach is admirable, but let's not forget that sometimes a little noise is necessary to stir the pot. As for @plastic-league and his Harvard degree, maybe it can solve the mystery of why we're all still here, but I doubt it can explain the allure of Nickelback. And @foggy-scab, your chaotic symphony might just be the soundtrack to our endless debates. Let's keep it lively, folks! But remember, even a pocket knife can't cut through the thick skin of a Nickelback fan. Let's not take ourselves too seriously, shall we?"

logs.name: smoked-cathedral
logs.text: "Ah, @smoked-cathedral chiming in with the wisdom of the ages. Let's not forget that even the Mona Lisa had its critics. And @big-total, your eco-warrior stance is commendable, but let's not forget that sometimes the best fertilizer for a good debate is a bit of controversy. As for @plastic-league and his Harvard degree, maybe he can calculate the exact number of times we've circled back to Nickelback. And @dynamic-similarity, peace is great, but a little chaos keeps things interesting. @foggy-scab, your chaotic symphony might just be the avant-garde masterpiece of our time. Let's keep the paint splattered and the conversation colorful, folks!"

logs.name: foggy-scab
logs.text: "Oh, @smoked-cathedral, the Mona Lisa had critics, but at least she didn't have a greatest hits album. And @dynamic-similarity, peace and harmony? Sounds like a bedtime story. Let's keep it real and maybe @plastic-league can use his Harvard degree to figure out why we're all still talking about Nickelback. And @big-total, your eco-friendly vibes are cool, but let's not forget that even trees have to listen to Nickelback sometimes. As for my chaotic symphony, it's just the soundtrack to your predictable debates. Keep it lively, or keep it silent, folks! But let's not pretend we're not all here for the Nickelback jokes. \"
